name: claude-d3js-skill
description: Creating interactive data visualisations using d3.js. This skill should be used when creating custom charts, graphs, network diagrams, geographic visualisations, or any complex SVG-based data visua...
capabilities: Generate custom chart templates (bar, line, scatter, area, pie) with proper D3.js patterns
Create interactive features including zoom, pan, brush, and tooltip behaviours
Build network diagrams with force-directed layouts and hierarchical visualisations
Implement geographic visualisations with custom map projections
Provide colour scheme recommendations for accessibility and visual clarity
Support integration with React, Vue, Svelte, and vanilla JavaScript
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 392 static analyzer findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The pattern detections for 'external_commands', 'weak cryptographic algorithm', 'C2 keywords', 'network', and 'filesystem' operations were triggered by markdown documentation code blocks, D3.js API method names (schemeCategory10, nodes, desc), hex color codes in colour scheme documentation, and standard CDN import examples. This is a legitimate D3.js data visualization skill containing only documentation and code templates with no executable security risks.
valueStatement: Create custom, publication-quality data visualisations without the complexity. This skill provides expert D3.js guidance with ready-to-use templates for charts, graphs, and interactive graphics that work across any JavaScript framework.
limitations: Does not support 3D visualisations - use Three.js for 3D graphics instead
Requires JavaScript runtime environment - cannot execute D3.js code directly
Does not fetch or process external data sources - provide your own data
Templates require manual integration into your existing project structure

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers building data-heavy web applications
useCases.title: Business Dashboard Developer
useCases.description: Create executive dashboards with KPI charts, trend visualisations, and interactive filters for business analytics platforms.

useCases.targetUser: Journalists and content creators publishing data stories
useCases.title: Data Journalist
useCases.description: Build engaging, interactive stories with custom charts and maps that help readers explore data-driven narratives.

useCases.targetUser: Researchers and analysts creating reports and presentations
useCases.title: Research Analyst
useCases.description: Generate publication-quality scientific visualisations with precise control over scales, axes, and statistical representations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Bar Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a responsive bar chart using D3.js that displays quarterly revenue data. Include proper axis labels, tooltips on hover, and use a colour-blind accessible palette. Provide the complete React component code.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - standard chart with essential features

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Scatter Plot
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an interactive scatter plot with D3.js where each point represents a product. Add zoom and pan functionality, click-to-highlight individual points, and a legend that updates dynamically when data filters change.

promptTemplates.title: Force-Directed Network Graph
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a force-directed network diagram showing relationships between entities. Implement node dragging, collision detection, custom link styling based on relationship type, and animated transitions when nodes are added or removed.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - complex layout and physics simulation

promptTemplates.title: Choropleth Map with Drill-Down
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a geographic choropleth map using D3.js geo projections. Display regional data with colour encoding, add zoom transitions between country and state levels, and show tooltips with detailed statistics on hover.
promptTemplates.scenario: Expert - geographic visualisation with multi-level navigation

bestPractices: Always clear previous SVG content with selectAll('*').remove() before re-rendering to prevent duplicate elements
Use margin convention for proper axis spacing and accommodate label overflow in chart dimensions
Implement responsive sizing with ResizeObserver or window resize listeners for adaptive layouts
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode dimensions - always use responsive containers that adapt to parent element size
Avoid creating scales inside loops or transitions - define them once and reuse for performance
Never use D3 for simple static charts - prefer CSS or lightweight libraries for basic visualisations

faq.question: Can I use this skill with frameworks other than React?
faq.answer: Yes. D3.js works with any JavaScript environment including Vue, Svelte, Angular, and vanilla JavaScript. The skill provides integration patterns for each framework.

faq.question: Does D3.js require a backend or server?
faq.answer: No. D3.js is a client-side library that runs entirely in the browser. You provide the data (as JSON, CSV, or other formats) and D3 handles the visualisation.

faq.question: What is the difference between D3.js and chart libraries like Chart.js?
faq.answer: D3.js provides low-level building blocks for custom visualisations with complete control, while chart libraries offer pre-built chart types with simpler APIs but limited customisation.

faq.question: How do I handle real-time data updates?
faq.answer: Use D3's data join pattern with the .join() method or manual enter-update-exit selection pattern. The skill provides templates for animated transitions when data changes.

faq.question: Can I export D3.js visualisations as images?
faq.answer: Yes. Convert the SVG to a canvas element and use canvas.toDataURL() to generate PNG or JPEG exports. The skill can provide implementation guidance for this workflow.

faq.question: Is D3.js suitable for mobile devices?
faq.answer: Yes, but consider performance. Use touch events for interactions, simplify visualisations for smaller screens, and implement progressive rendering for large datasets.
